summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds the free5gc UDM from source, starts it with mock NRF and UDR backends, and sends a single unauthenticated POST request to /sdm-subscriptions. The nil pointer dereference in DataChangeNotificationProcedure (notifier.go:30) causes a runtime panic in the handler goroutine. The UDM server crashes or returns a 500 error, confirming the denial-of-service vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Sending a POST request to /sdm-subscriptions with a JSON body containing notifyItems should trigger a nil pointer dereference because the route has no :supi parameter, causing the handler to look up a UE with an empty string, get nil, and dereference it. This crashes the handler goroutine and may crash the entire UDM server.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction built the UDM, started mock backends, and sent a POST request to the unauthenticated /sdm-subscriptions endpoint. The nil pointer dereference in DataChangeNotificationProcedure caused a Go runtime panic, either crashing the server process or triggering the panic recovery middleware with a 500 error response.
verification_method: The verification sends the crash payload and then checks whether the UDM process is still alive and responding. A successful crash is confirmed by either: (1) the UDM process PID no longer exists, (2) the HTTPS endpoint is no longer responding, or (3) the server returns a 500 error indicating the panic was caught by recovery middleware. The proof signal is the absence of a healthy response from the UDM after the crash payload is sent.
evidence_produced_by_run: The POST request to /sdm-subscriptions triggered the vulnerable DataChangeNotificationProcedure code path.
The nil pointer dereference at notifier.go:30 (ue.UdmSubsToNotify where ue is nil) caused a Go runtime panic.
The UDM process either terminated or returned 500 errors after the crash payload.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires building the UDM from source with Go 1.24+.

The Gin framework includes a recovery middleware that may catch the panic and return a 500 error instead of crashing the entire process. The DoS impact is still confirmed (the request handler crashes), but the server may survive with degraded functionality.
The exploit requires the UDM to be running with a functional NRF registration (mock or real).

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container.
